commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=812d3c0ac67e8a1d116da844e06bf6b481f17728)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an inline button JSON extractor, tests it, and wires it into `message` tool text sanitization to create interactive button blocks from model-generated text.

PR surface: Source +201, Tests +114. Total +315 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: the linked issue shows Gemini raw button text, while current automatic group prompts tell models not to use the same-group message tool. I did not run a live Gemini/Telegram reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to choose whether raw model-output JSON should become a supported contract; the current branch also has blocking parser and path-coverage defects.

target.title: fix(message-tool): parse inline [[...]] button JSON from model text output
target.body:
## Summary

- Some models (e.g. Gemini) output button definitions like `[[{"text":"Label","callback_data":"data"}]]` as raw text in the message body instead of using the interactive blocks parameter.
- Add `extractInlineButtons()` helper that scans text for double-bracket-wrapped JSON button arrays using bracket-aware scanning.
- Supports both single-row `[{...}]` and multi-row `[[{...}],[{...}]]` formats.
- Integrate extraction into the message tool text sanitization pipeline so inline buttons are converted to interactive blocks before delivery.

## Verification

- 9 unit test cases covering: single button, multi-button single row, multi-row, TTS directive safety, multiple blocks, empty input, invalid JSON, and style preservation.
- All tests passing via `node --import tsx` verification.
- Existing [[tts:voice]] and [[reply_to_current]] directive parsing is unaffected.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Models that emit inline button JSON as text instead of using the interactive blocks parameter now have those buttons extracted and converted to proper interactive blocks.

**Environment tested:** Node.js v22.22.0 on Linux x64, openclaw main branch.

**Steps run after the patch:** Called `extractInlineButtons()` from source via node --import tsx with 9 test scenarios.

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
PASS: single button
PASS: no buttons
PASS: multi buttons single row
PASS: multi row
PASS: tts directives ignored
PASS: multiple blocks
PASS: empty
PASS: invalid json ignored
PASS: style preserved

9/9 passed
```

**Observed result after fix:** All 9 test cases pass. Single-row and multi-row button formats are correctly extracted. TTS directives like [[tts:voice]] and [[reply_to_current]] are unchanged. Multiple inline button blocks in the same text are all extracted.

**Not tested:** Live gateway integration with a real Gemini model sending inline button text through the full reply dispatch pipeline.

Closes #41495
target.author: crh-code
